dashboard.label: T1046 — Network Service Discovery
dashboard.description: TCP port-scan detection. Aggregates Zeek conn.log; flags (src, dest) pairs with high distinct-port cardinality and incomplete-handshake fraction.

search.query: `network_service_discovery` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval window=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") | sort -distinct_ports | table window, src, dest, distinct_ports, total_connections, incomplete_fraction, duration_seconds, sample_ports

panel.title: Distinct ports per (src, dest), top scanners

search.query: index=zeek sourcetype=zeek:conn | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | bin _time span=60s | stats dc('id.resp_p') AS distinct_ports BY _time, src, 'id.resp_h' | rename "id.resp_h" AS dest | where distinct_ports >= 20 | eval pair=src . " -> " . dest | timechart span=60s max(distinct_ports) BY pair limit=8

html.p: After a foothold, attackers map reachable services with a TCP SYN scan (nmap -sS, masscan). The probe never completes the handshake — Zeek records it as S0, REJ, or one of the RST* states.
The detection groups Zeek conn.log records by (60-s window, src, dest) and requires both distinct dest-port count ≥ 100 and incomplete-handshake fraction ≥ 0.7.
